What is ShutterMuse?

ShutterMuse is a multimodal large language model that provides photography guidance at the moment of capture, advising both the photographer on framing and the human subject on posing before the picture is taken.

What is shift-variant blur?
It is blur whose point-spread function changes from place to place in the image, so the degradation must be treated as a spatially varying linear operator rather than one fixed kernel.

How does this method restore shift-variant blurred images?
It decomposes the degradation operator with the singular value decomposition and inverts only the well-conditioned large singular values while suppressing the small ones that would amplify noise.

Why use the SVD for shift-variant deblurring?

Because the shift-variant degradation operator is severely ill-conditioned; the singular value decomposition exposes which singular values are safe to invert and which would amplify noise if inverted.
